Title: Underwater CAM photosynthesis elucidated by Isoetes genome

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: night-time carbon sequestration for underwater CAM, reducing photorespiration under low dissolved CO2
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: phosphoenolpyruvate carboxylase; includes plant-type and bacterial-type PEPC clades
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: both plant-type and bacterial-type PEPC copies cycle; both peak at dusk and taper through the night; bacterial-type expressed much higher than plant-type
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: both plant-type and bacterial-type PEPC copies cycle; both peak at dusk and taper through the night; bacterial-type expressed much higher than plant-type
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: submerged growth; 12 h light/12 h dark under constant temperature (LDHH); underwater low CO2 context
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: likely contributes to underwater CAM carboxylation, with unusual strong involvement of bacterial-type PEPC
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PEPC is the entry-point carboxylation enzyme for CAM in I. taiwanensis. 2. Both plant-type and bacterial-type PEPC genes cycle in diel expression. 3. Bacterial-type PEPC is expressed at much higher levels than plant-type PEPC, unlike previously characterized CAM/C4 plants. 4. No convergent CAM-associated Asp substitution was found in Isoetes PEPC copies.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: time-course RNA-seq; phylogenetic analysis of PEPC family; sequence comparison of active-site-associated residue; metabolite/acid titration context
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PPCK; bacterial-type PEPC may form hetero-octameric complex with plant-type PEPC
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq time course, NCBI BioProject PRJNA735564
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper discusses PEPC extensively as a focal CAM gene but does not functionally separate individual PEPC isoforms by transgenics or enzyme assay in vivo; authors emphasize possible recruitment of bacterial-type PEPC.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports nocturnal carbon fixation during underwater CAM
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports canonical night-phase CAM regulation via dark expression
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PPCK shows strong dark expression in I. taiwanensis. 2. This expression pattern is conserved relative to terrestrial CAM taxa. 3. PPCK supports similarity between underwater and terrestrial CAM regulation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: time-course RNA-seq comparison with terrestrial CAM species
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq time course, NCBI BioProject PRJNA735564
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used here mainly as supportive CAM marker rather than uniquely novel target.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Crassulacean acid metabolism (CAM) / carbon concentrating metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: reversibly hydrates CO2 to bicarbonate, supplying substrate pool for PEPC-mediated carboxylation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: night-time carbon storage for daytime assimilation under low underwater CO2
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: one copy cycled, increasing during the night and peaking in the early morning
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: one copy cycled, increasing during the night and peaking in the early morning
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: submerged growth; 12 h light/12 h dark; constant temperature
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports nocturnal CAM carboxylation through bicarbonate provision
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. One β-CA copy cycles in I. taiwanensis. 2. Its expression increases through the night and peaks in early morning. 3. This pattern resembles pineapple but differs from S. album.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq time course, NCBI BioProject PRJNA735564
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be paired with PEPC/PPCK for engineering nocturnal carbon capture, but this paper provides expression evidence only.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter searches for known circadian motifs in CAM genes found no strong canonical enrichment pattern.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: night fixation and day decarboxylation-associated malate/OAA interconversion module
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: temporary nocturnal carbon storage for daytime CO2 release
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: multiple copies cycle; one copy shows similar timing to pineapple ortholog; additional cycling copies show divergent timing
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: multiple copies cycle; one copy shows similar timing to pineapple ortholog; additional cycling copies show divergent timing
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports malate formation and may participate in MDH/PEPCK daytime decarboxylation route
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Multiple MDH copies cycle in I. taiwanensis. 2. One cycling MDH resembles the timing of pineapple CAM MDH. 3. Additional MDH paralogs display divergent diel expression. 4. Data support possible use of an MDH/PEPCK decarboxylation pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq time course, NCBI BioProject PRJNA735564
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential component for engineering reversible nocturnal malate flux, especially in combination with PEPCK.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors infer pathway preference from expression dynamics rather than direct enzyme flux assays.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: decarboxylates and phosphorylates oxaloacetate to phosphoenolpyruvate, releasing CO2-equivalent flux for daytime assimilation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: releases stored carbon toward Calvin cycle
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: enables daytime recovery of carbon stored at night in malate
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: cycling copy elevated during the day; similar TOD expression to pineapple PEPCK though not orthologous
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: cycling copy elevated during the day; similar TOD expression to pineapple PEPCK though not orthologous
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: implicated in daytime CAM decarboxylation and suggests preference for MDH/PEPCK pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PEPCK is day-expressed in I. taiwanensis. 2. Together with MDH expression, it suggests use of the MDH/PEPCK decarboxylation pathway. 3. The cycling PEPCK copy is not orthologous to the cycling pineapple copy.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq time course, NCBI BioProject PRJNA735564
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate day-phase decarboxylation gene for CAM engineering, though pathway assignment here is inferential.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Inference based on expression; no direct decarboxylation flux measurements.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: oxidatively decarboxylates malate to pyruvate and CO2
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: alternative route releasing stored carbon toward Calvin cycle
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: stored night carbon intermediate of CAM
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: elevated during the day but expression pattern inverted compared with K. fedtschenkoi and S. album
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: elevated during the day but expression pattern inverted compared with K. fedtschenkoi and S. album
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: less likely to define the preferred daytime decarboxylation route in I. taiwanensis compared with MDH/PEPCK
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NADP-ME is day-expressed in I. taiwanensis. 2. Its diel profile is inverted relative to some terrestrial CAM species. 3. This weakens support for NADP-ME/PPDK as the dominant daytime route.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq time course, NCBI BioProject PRJNA735564
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be a secondary decarboxylation option; caution because expression differs from canonical terrestrial CAM profiles.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not excluded from CAM role, but paper favors MDH/PEPCK route.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: converts pyruvate to phosphoenolpyruvate during regeneration of carboxylation substrate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: recycles precursor pool for continued CAM flux
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: elevated during the day but overall weak cycling (R = 0.637)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: elevated during the day but overall weak cycling (R = 0.637)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: appears not to be the dominant strongly cycling daytime CAM component in I. taiwanensis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PPDK shows only weak diel cycling in I. taiwanensis. 2. This contrasts with stronger dependence on NADP-ME/PPDK in some terrestrial CAM plants. 3. Findings support alternative decarboxylation preference.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq time course, NCBI BioProject PRJNA735564
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Probably not the best standalone target for reproducing Isoetes-like underwater CAM based on this study.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: night-time carbon storage associated with CAM
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: circadian timing of CAM-associated gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: part of core circadian clock network
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: supportive clock component; not shown as decisive CAM-specific switch
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: cycles with expected time-of-day pattern similar to Arabidopsis ortholog
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: cycles with expected time-of-day pattern similar to Arabidopsis ortholog
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 12 h light/12 h dark; constant temperature; submerged growth
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: indicates conserved core clock operation in I. taiwanensis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LHY cycles with expected TOD expression. 2. This supports conservation of part of the core clock in Isoetes. 3. The paper uses it as a circadian benchmark for CAM timing context.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq time course, NCBI BioProject PRJNA735564
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: General clock engineering target, but no direct CAM metabolite engineering value established here.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because circadian regulation is a major theme, though direct CAM gene targeting was not demonstrated.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: circadian timing of CAM and other diel gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: part of core circadian feedback loops
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: condition-dependent controller; unusual phase shift suggests altered CAM clock architecture
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: three full-length paralogs; all show dawn-specific expression instead of canonical dusk-specific expression
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: three full-length paralogs; all show dawn-specific expression instead of canonical dusk-specific expression
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 12 h light/12 h dark; constant temperature; submerged growth
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: reveals major divergence of clock phase architecture potentially linked to underwater CAM regulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. I. taiwanensis has three PRR1/TOC1 paralogs. 2. All exhibit dawn-specific expression, unlike dusk-specific expression in other tested plants. 3. This suggests divergent circadian regulation associated with Isoetes biology and possibly underwater CAM.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: time-course RNA-seq; genomic copy-number inspection; comparative expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq time course, NCBI BioProject PRJNA735564
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Interesting for reprogramming diel metabolism, but strong pleiotropy is likely and direct engineering value remains uncertain.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct regulation of CAM genes was not experimentally demonstrated.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: circadian timing network influencing diel metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: supports altered clock architecture rather than directly proven CAM switch
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: five predicted genes; one full-length GIa with dusk-specific expression and one truncated expressed GIb with dawn-specific antiphasic expression
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: five predicted genes; one full-length GIa with dusk-specific expression and one truncated expressed GIb with dawn-specific antiphasic expression
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 12 h light/12 h dark; constant temperature; submerged growth
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: indicates lineage-specific duplication and phase diversification in circadian control that may relate to underwater CAM timing
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GI is expanded in I. taiwanensis. 2. GIa retains dusk-specific expression, while truncated GIb is dawn-specific and antiphasic. 3. These patterns imply divergence in circadian regulation relative to other plants.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: time-course RNA-seq; genomic inspection of paralogs; comparative expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq time course, NCBI BioProject PRJNA735564
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential clock rewiring node for diel metabolism, but direct metabolic output effects were not tested.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Role in CAM remains inferential through clock architecture rather than direct target-gene assays.
